source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: We employ a semiempirical approach combining climate model simulations and observational temperatures
We find the 2024 global temperature record to have been unremarkable when anthropogenic forcing is accounted for
Only 1998 was found to be truly anomalous among recent record-breaking years
These conclusions are robust to screening hot models from the CMIP6 ensemble
Recent record warmth does not contradict state-of-the-art climate model historical simulations
Such models continue to provide accurate assessments of planetary warming and a sound basis for climate policymaking

typical_pattern: State main finding with quantitative likelihood
Compare to other record years with specific probabilities
Conclude with broader implications for policy or field

likely_priorities: Robustness of conclusions to model selection and screening criteria
Accounting for recent unanticipated changes in radiative forcing
Validity of emissions scenario assumptions for recent historical period
Consistency with independent assessments of climate variability
likely_attacks: Historical CMIP6 simulations are based on projections after 2015 and may not account for recent forcing changes
Hunga Tonga eruption radiative forcing may not be negligible
Ship-borne sulfur emissions policy changes may have larger warming effects than estimated
SSP2-4.5 projections may not accurately characterize recent historical emissions
Monte Carlo approach may underestimate or overestimate true likelihood of extremes
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The paper explicitly addresses multiple objections in the conclusion section: it discusses the limitation that CMIP6 simulations are based on projections after 2015, then systematically rebuts concerns about Hunga Tonga eruption forcing, ship-borne sulfur emissions, and emissions scenario assumptions. The phrase 'One objection that might be raised' signals anticipation of reviewer concerns. The paper also emphasizes robustness to hot model screening and notes that SSP2-4.5 is 'arguably conservative,' suggesting awareness that reviewers might question these methodological choices.

examples.quoted_phrase: We find the El Niño-boosted 2024 global temperature record to have been unremarkable
These conclusions are robust to screening hot models from the CMIP6 ensemble
Recent studies, however, suggest that the radiative forcing from this eruption was negligible
we find no evidence for any departure from the expectations of typical natural climate variability

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. Quantitative values (12%, 14%, 2.5%, one-in-eight-year, one in forty-year, +0.09 Wm−2, <0.03 °C) appear verbatim in the input. No external references, author names, or invented content included. The paper provides sufficient detail in abstract and conclusion for a complete profile.
